product.positioning: Wallet-native acute emergency travel protection for nomads, residents, and longer travel windows.

pricing.sponsorPricingNote: Sponsor pricing is negotiated for larger cohorts.

tiers.label: Tier 1
tiers.trigger: ER or urgent hospital evaluation with same-day discharge

reimbursementTopUp.description: Actual acute emergency medical spend above the fixed tier benefit, capped at the aggregate maximum.

coveredWhen: Acute, unplanned, and medically necessary within the cover window.
Sudden acute illness needing ER or inpatient care.
Ambulance tied to the covered acute event.
Emergency imaging, labs, procedures, medications, and hospital fees tied to the covered acute event.
exclusions: pregnancy, maternity, fertility, and neonatal care
preventive care, screening, wellness checks, and standard medication refills
dental, optical, and hearing care unless a later trauma rider is added
evacuation, repatriation, baggage, and trip cancellation
substance-related events, self-harm, fraud, criminal acts, and sanctions-excluded scenarios

claimsTrustModel.phase0: OmegaX Health operator-backed oracle flow plus hidden concierge review.
claimsTrustModel.phase1: AI claim recommendation with human override kept private when needed.
claimsTrustModel.phase2: Explicit claims operators with governance-visible dispute handling.

commercialLayers.appMembership: Separate OmegaX Health app subscription for AI health agent value and premium app features.
commercialLayers.protectionPremium: Per-window premium that activates Travel 30 protection eligibility in the protocol.

premium.reserveRole: Collected premiums attributed to the Travel 30 protection reserve lane.

liquidity.reserveRole: Posted LP and backstop capital that supports Travel 30 obligations.

issuanceControls.reserveAttribution: Only posted capital, collected premiums, and explicit backstop funds count as claims-paying reserve.
issuanceControls.publicStatusRule: Keep Travel 30 as the primary launch SKU, but do not market it as broadly live insurance until reserve posting and operator readiness are both active.
issueWhen: Travel 30 reserve lanes are funded onchain with published premium and liquidity support.
Operator-backed claim review is staffed for the active issuance window.
The shared public evidence schema stays aligned with the published metadata document.
pauseWhen: Posted reserve no longer covers the marketed issuance window.
Claims operator or oracle staffing falls below the required phase-0 review readiness.
Schema, pricing, or benefit truth drifts from the published Genesis metadata.
